description: Local PRD tracker stories for Kollywood dogfooding.

acceptanceCriteria: Recent activity rows on overview are readable on mobile without overflow
Clicking a recent activity row navigates to the correct run detail page
Clicking a story ID or title in the Stories view opens a slide-over with full story details
Slide-over can be closed with a button or backdrop click
Each story card has a status change control that updates prd.json on disk
Run detail page auto-expands run.log and worker.log panels if they have content
Settings section shows workflow_path and tracker_path
Tests cover show_story and update_story_status events
userStories.description:
Polish the project dashboard across all sections. Key changes:

1. **Overview - Recent Activity (mobile fix):** The activity row `flex` layout overflows on small screens. Change to a stacked layout on mobile: status badge + ID/title on top row, error (if any) on second row beneath. Use `flex-col sm:flex-row` or similar.

2. **Overview - Clickable items:** Each recent activity row should navigate to the run detail for that story's latest attempt. Use `navigate` to `/projects/:slug/runs/:story_id/:attempt` where attempt = `story["lastAttempt"]`. Wrap each row in a `<.link>`.

3. **Stories - Story detail slide-over:** Clicking a story ID badge or card title should open a slide-over panel (right side, overlay) showing: title, status badge, description (full), acceptance criteria (bulleted list), notes, dependsOn badges, priority, lastError if any. Add a close button. Implement as a new `story_detail` component toggled by a `selected_story` assign (set via `handle_event("show_story", %{"id" => id}, socket)`). No new route needed - use socket assigns.

4. **Stories - Status quick-change:** Add a small dropdown or set of buttons on each story card to change its status (open / in_progress / done / failed / cancelled). On change, update prd.json on disk and reassign `@stories`. Wire a `handle_event("update_story_status", %{"id" => id, "status" => status}, socket)`. Read current prd.json, find the story, update status, write back, reload stories.

5. **Runs - Log visibility:** In the run detail view, the log collapse panels exist but may be hard to find. Ensure the run detail is accessible: from the Runs list, each row should have a clear 'View' link. In the run detail, auto-expand the `run.log` panel. If `worker.log` has content, show it expanded too. If all logs are empty strings, show a 'No logs available' message instead of nothing.

6. **Settings - show workflow path and tracker path:** Add two read-only fields showing `workflow_path` and `tracker_path` from the project record, so users know which files are being used.

Implementation notes:
- Stories are read from `project.tracker_path` (a prd.json file on disk). Writes should use `File.write!/2` with `Jason.encode!(..., pretty: true)`.
- The slide-over should use a fixed overlay div with `z-50`, a backdrop click to close, and slide in from the right with a Tailwind `translate-x` transition.
- Add `id` attributes to key elements per Phoenix LiveView best practices.
- Keep existing DaisyUI styling consistent.
- Add tests for the new `show_story` and `update_story_status` events in `dashboard_live_test.exs`.

userStories.title: Improve dashboard UI: story details, run logs, and mobile layout

acceptanceCriteria: agent.log file is created in each attempt directory alongside worker.log and run.log
agent.log contains only raw Claude output text, one block per turn separated by '--- Turn N ---'
worker.log and run.log continue to contain only orchestrator lifecycle events (no change to existing behaviour)
Run detail view shows Agent | Run | Checks | Reviewer | Runtime tab bar
Agent tab is selected by default and shows agent.log content
Each tab shows the corresponding log file content in a monospace pre block
When the run status is 'running', log content refreshes every 1 second
Log pre block auto-scrolls to the bottom as new content arrives
Polling stops automatically when run status changes from 'running'
Empty log files show 'No output yet' placeholder instead of blank space
Tests cover agent.log population and live polling behaviour
userStories.description:
Two related improvements to run log visibility: ## Part 1: Separate agent.log from lifecycle events Currently `worker.log` and `run.log` contain only orchestrator lifecycle events (`turn_started`, `turn_succeeded`, etc.). The actual Claude agent output is embedded inside the `output` field of `turn_succeeded` events and never written to a dedicated file. Add a dedicated `agent.log` file to the run attempt directory that contains only the raw agent stdout — the actual Claude conversation, tool calls, and code output. **Backend changes in `lib/kollywood/orchestrator/run_logs.ex`:** - Add `agent: Path.join(attempt_dir, "agent.log")` to `build_attempt_files/2` - Add `files.agent` to `ensure_append_files/1` - Add `files.agent` to `initial_metadata/8` files map - Add `files.agent` to `tracker_metadata/1` return - In `append_event/2`: when event type is `turn_succeeded` or `review_passed` or `review_failed`, extract the `output` field value and append it to `files.agent` as raw text (not a structured log line). Prepend a separator line `\n--- Turn N ---\n` using the turn number from the event before each block. **No changes needed to `cli.ex` or `agent_runner.ex`** — the output is already captured in the event payload. ## Part 2: Real-time log streaming in the UI Add a GitHub Actions / GitLab CI style live log viewer in `DashboardLive` run detail. **LiveView changes in `lib/kollywood_web/live/dashboard_live.ex`:** 1. **Log tab layout:** Replace the current collapse panels in `run_detail_section` with a tab bar: `Agent` | `Run` | `Checks` | `Reviewer` | `Runtime`. Default to the `Agent` tab. Each tab shows the content of the corresponding log file in a `
` block with `id="log-output"` and class `font-mono text-xs leading-relaxed bg-base-300 p-4 rounded-lg overflow-auto max-h-[70vh] whitespace-pre-wrap`.

2. **Active run polling:** When mounting/handling params for `:run_detail`, check if the run's metadata status is `"running"`. If so, start a 1-second interval timer via `:timer.send_interval(1000, self(), :poll_logs)`. Store the current log content length in `@log_poll_offset` and which tab is active in `@active_log_tab` (default `"agent"`).

3. **`handle_info(:poll_logs, socket)`:** Re-read the active log file from disk, assign updated content to `@run_detail`. Stop polling (cancel timer stored in `@log_poll_timer`) when metadata status is no longer `"running"`. Re-read metadata.json each poll cycle to check status.

4. **Tab switching:** Add `handle_event("set_log_tab", %{"tab" => tab}, socket)` that sets `@active_log_tab` and re-reads the corresponding log file.

5. **Auto-scroll JS hook:** Add a colocated JS hook named `.LogScroll` on the `
` element. In `updated()`, scroll the element to its `scrollHeight`. This makes it follow new output like a terminal.

6. **Empty state:** If the selected log file is empty or missing, show `
No output yet.
` instead of an empty pre block.

**The polling reads the log file directly from disk** using `File.read/1` on the path stored in `run_detail["files"][tab_key]` (e.g. `run_detail["files"]["agent"]`). The `load_run_detail/3` function already builds this map — extend it to include the raw file contents for the active tab.

## Implementation notes
- The `run_detail` assign in DashboardLive currently holds a map from `load_run_detail/3`. Extend it to include `"active_log_content"` key populated from the active tab's log file.
- Timer refs: store poll timer ref in socket assigns as `@log_poll_timer` (a reference from `:timer.send_interval`). Cancel it with `:timer.cancel/1` when navigating away (`handle_params`) or when status is no longer running.
- Add `handle_event("set_log_tab", ...)` and `handle_info(:poll_logs, ...)` to `DashboardLive`.
- Add tests: verify `agent.log` file is created and contains turn output after `append_event` with a `turn_succeeded` event; verify `handle_info(:poll_logs)` updates log content; verify tab switching changes active content.

userStories.title: Live log streaming and agent log separation in run detail view

acceptanceCriteria: Draft stories appear in the Stories view under a 'Draft' section, visually distinct from active stories
Draft badge uses a neutral/ghost style to distinguish from active statuses
Draft stories are excluded from the Overview stat cards (open/in_progress/done/failed counts)
Status change control includes 'draft' as an option alongside open/in_progress/done/failed/cancelled
Promoting a draft story to open via the status control makes it immediately eligible for dispatch
WORKFLOW.md active_states and terminal_states do not include draft — orchestrator continues to ignore draft stories
userStories.description:
Add `draft` as a first-class story status in the dashboard UI.

Draft stories exist in prd.json but are intentionally not dispatched — they are ideas or planned work that isn't ready yet. The orchestrator already ignores them (draft is not in active_states or terminal_states). The UI should show them clearly.

## Changes in `dashboard_live.ex`

1. **Stories section:** Add a `Draft` group below the existing status groups. Show draft stories with a ghost/neutral badge and slightly muted card styling to make clear they are not active.

2. **Stat cards:** Keep the four overview cards as open/in_progress/done/failed. Do NOT add a draft counter there — draft is not actionable work.

3. **Status badge:** Add a `draft` case to `status_badge/1` using `badge-ghost` or similar neutral DaisyUI class.

4. **Status change control (from US-112):** Include `draft` as an option so stories can be demoted back to draft or promoted from draft to open.

## No backend changes needed
- The orchestrator already ignores draft (not in active_states)
- prd.json writes already handle arbitrary status strings via `update_story_status` event from US-112
userStories.title: Show draft stories in dashboard UI as a distinct non-active group

acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator claim/running/completed state survives a server restart without re-dispatching stories
Resetting a story to open in the UI causes the orchestrator to re-dispatch it on next poll without a restart
A new Ecto migration adds an orchestrator_runs table with story_id, attempt, status, started_at, ended_at
On startup, the orchestrator reads active runs from SQLite to restore running/completed state
On dispatch, a run row is inserted; on completion/failure, the row is updated
Tests cover restart recovery and reset-then-redispatch behaviour
userStories.description:
The orchestrator currently tracks `running`, `completed`, and `claimed` state in in-memory MapSets. This is lost on every server restart, causing stories to be re-dispatched unexpectedly.

Persist orchestrator run state to SQLite so restarts are safe.

## Schema

Add a migration for an `orchestrator_runs` table:
- `story_id` string (indexed)
- `attempt` integer
- `status` string — `running | done | failed | stopped`
- `started_at` utc_datetime
- `ended_at` utc_datetime (nullable)
- `error` text (nullable)

## Behaviour changes

**On startup:** query `orchestrator_runs` for rows with `status = running`. For each, check the current story status in the tracker. If the story is still `in_progress`, add it to `completed` (treat as orphaned — do not re-dispatch). If the story has been reset to `open`, leave it out of `completed` so it gets re-dispatched normally.

**On dispatch:** insert a row with `status = running`.

**On completion/failure:** update the row to `done` or `failed` with `ended_at`.

**On reset to open:** the existing `clear_completed_for_open_issues/2` poll-cycle hook already handles the in-memory side. The SQLite row for the previous run is left as historical record — do not delete it.

## Implementation notes
- Add `Kollywood.OrchestratorRun` Ecto schema and migration.
- Keep the in-memory MapSets — SQLite is only consulted on startup and written on state transitions. Hot path stays fast.
- No changes needed to `prd_json.ex` or tracker adapters.
userStories.title: Persist orchestrator run state to SQLite for restart safety

acceptanceCriteria: lastAttempt in prd.json is updated to the new attempt number when mark_in_progress is called
Overview recent activity links navigate to the correct (current) attempt
Stories page shows the correct attempt number badge for in_progress stories
lastAttempt is consistent with the actual attempt directories in .kollywood/run_logs
userStories.description:
The `lastAttempt` field in prd.json is only written on failure or completion, never when a new attempt starts. This causes the overview and stories pages to show a stale attempt number while a run is active.

## Fix

In `mark_in_progress/2` in `prd_json.ex`, increment and write `lastAttempt` atomically with the status change. The attempt number should come from the orchestrator — thread it through `Tracker.mark_in_progress/3` (add an `attempt` argument) and update the behaviour spec and all callers in `orchestrator.ex`.

Alternatively, derive the attempt count from the run_logs directory in `list_run_attempts/1` and pass it to `mark_in_progress`. Either approach is fine as long as prd.json reflects the actual current attempt when status is `in_progress`.
